rules.name: One feature per session
rules.trigger: User hands a new, unrelated feature after finishing or deferring one.
rules.rule: A session covers one feature slug (or one tightly-coupled slug group). Propose starting a fresh session rather than stacking.
rules.check: If plan.md's current-focus section would need to change slug, it's a new session.

rules.name: Todo hygiene at every checkpoint
rules.rule: Reap stale rows from the `todos` table. Keep active count under ~15 rows.
rules.check: `SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM todos GROUP BY status` stays under ~15 total rows on any active day.
rules.fix: Run `scripts/sql/reap-stale-todos.sql` with the current feature slug bound.

rules.name: plan.md current-focus is mandatory and time-stamped
rules.rule: Rewrite the Active-work 5-line block with current timestamp. No 2-hour gap on an active day.
rules.check: If the conversation summary is newer than plan.md's timestamp by > 30 min, first act on resume is to reconcile.

rules.name: Retry budget on long cargo commands
rules.trigger: A cargo/tauri command > 2 min fails with an identical error hash.
rules.rule: Do not re-run a third time without a strategy change (`cargo clean`, scope swap, live-app pivot, ask user). Log the pivot under plan.md > Retry log.
rules.fix: Append a one-line entry per `.github/templates/retry-log-entry.md` with command, error, and switch strategy.

rules.name: Async shell accounting
rules.trigger: Ending any turn with an async shell still running.
rules.rule: Either `stop_powershell` it, OR record shellId + PID + purpose under plan.md > Live shells.
rules.check: On session resume, first act is `list_powershell` and reap orphans.
rules.fix: Stale async shells hold target/debug/*.dll locks and cause DLL-race failures — stop or record.

split_session_triggers: Starting a feature whose slug differs from plan.md's Active work block.
`plan.md` timestamp is > 4 hours old and the session is still active.
Conversation summary has been auto-compacted more than once.
More than 2 async shells are running.
